author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew IX. 1, 2.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And He entered into a ship, and passed over, and came into His own city.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And, behold, they brought to Him a man sick of the palsy, lying on a bed: and Jesus seeing their faith said unto the sick of the palsy; Son, be of good cheer; thy sins be forgiven thee.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By His own city here he means Capernaum.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For that which gave Him birth was Bethlehem; that which brought Him up, Nazareth; that which had Him continually inhabiting it, Capernaum.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This paralytic, however, was different from that one who is set forth in John.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For he lay at the pool, but this at Capernaum; and that man had his infirmity thirty and eight years, but concerning this, no such thing is mentioned; and the other was in a state destitute of protectors, but this had some to take care of him, who also took him up, and carried him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to this He saith, “Son, thy sins be forgiven thee,”but to that He saith, “Wilt thou be made whole?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the other He healed on a sabbath day, but this not on a sabbath, for else the Jews would have laid this also to His charge; and in the case of this man they were silent, but in that of the other they were instant in persecuting him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this I have said, not without purpose, lest any one should think there is a discrepancy from suspecting it to be one and the same paralytic.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But do thou, I pray thee, mark the humility and meekness of our Lord.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He had also before this put away the multitudes from Him, and moreover when sent away by them at Gadara, He withstood not, but retired, not however to any great distance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again He entered into the ship and passed over, when He might have gone over afoot.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it was His will not to be always doing miracles, that He might not injure the doctrine of His humanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now Matthew indeed saith, that “they brought him,” but the others, that they also broke up the roof, and let him down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they put the sick man before Christ, saying nothing, but committing the whole to Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though in the beginning He Himself went about, and did not require so much faith of them that came unto Him; yet in this case they both approached Him, and had faith required on their part.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Seeing,” it is said, “their faith;” that is, the faith of them that had let the man down.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He doth not on all occasions require faith on the part of the sick only: as for instance, when they are insane, or in any other way, through their disease, are out of their own control.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or rather, in this case the sick man too had part in the faith; for he would not have suffered himself to be let down, unless he had believed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Forasmuch then as they had evinced so great faith, He also evinces His own power, with all authority absolving his sins, and signifying in all ways that He is equal in honor with Him that begat Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark; He implied it from the beginning, by His teaching, when He taught them as one having authority; by the leper, when He said, “I will, be thou clean,”by the centurion, when upon his saying, “Speak the word only, and my servant shall be healed, He marvelled at him,”and celebrated him above all men; by the sea, when He curbed it with a mere word; by the devils, when they acknowledged Him as their judge, and He cast them out with great authority.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Here again in another and a greater way He constrains His very enemies to confess His equality in honor, and by their own mouth He makes it manifest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He, to signify His indifference to honor (for there stood a great company of spectators shutting up the entrance, wherefore also they let him down from above), did not straightway hasten to heal the visible body, but He takes His occasion from them; and He healed first that which is invisible, the soul, by forgiving his sins; which indeed saved the other, but brought no great glory to Himself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: They themselves rather, troubled by their malice, and wishing to assail Him, caused even against their will what was done to be conspicuous.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He, in fact, in His abundance of counsel, made use of their envy for the manifestation of the miracle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Upon their murmuring,then, and saying, “This man blasphemeth; who can forgive sins but God only?”let us see what He saith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Did He indeed take away the suspicion?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet if He were not equal, He should have said, “Why fix upon me a notion which is not convenient?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I am far from this power.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now hath He said none of these things, but quite the contrary He hath both affirmed and ratified, as well by His own voice, as by the performance of the miracle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, it appearing that His saying certain things of Himself gave disgust to his hearers, He affirms what He had to say concerning Himself by the others; and what is truly marvellous, not by His friends only, but also by His enemies; for this is the excellency of His wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: By His friends on the one hand, when He said, “I will, be thou clean,”and when He said, “I have not found so great faith, no, not in Israel;”but by His enemies, now.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For because they had said, “No man can forgive sins but God only,” He subjoined,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But that ye may know that the Son of Man hath power to forgive sins upon the earth (then saith He to the sick of the palsy), Arise, and take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not here only, but also in another case again, when they were saying, “For a good work we stone thee not, but for blasphemy, and because that thou, being a man, makest thyself God,”neither in that instance did He put down this opinion, but again confirmed it, saying, “If I do not the works of my Father, believe me not; but if I do, though ye believe not me, believe the works.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: In this case indeed He discloses also another sign, and that no small one, of His own Godhead, and of His equality in honor with the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas they said, “To unbind sins pertains to God only,” He not only unbinds sins, but also before this He makes another kind of display in a thing which pertained to God only; the publishing the secrets in the heart.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither had they uttered what they were thinking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For “behold, certain of the scribes,” it saith, “said within themselves, This man blasphemeth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Jesus knowing their thoughts, said, Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But that it belongs to God only to know men’s secrets, hear what saith the prophet, “Thou most entirely aloneknowest the hearts;”and again, “God trieth the hearts and reins;” and Jeremiah too saith, “The heart is deep above all things, and it is man, and who shall know him?”and, “Man shall look on the face, but God on the heart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And by many things one may see, that to know what is in the mind belongs to God alone.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Implying therefore that He is God, equal to Him that begat Him; what things they were reasoning in themselves (for through fear of the multitude, they durst not utter their mind), this their opinion He unveils and makes manifest, evincing herein also His great gentleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For wherefore,” saith He, “think ye evil in your hearts?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And yet if there were cause for displeasure, it was the sick man who should have been displeased, as being altogether deceived, and should have said “One thing I came to have healed, and amendest Thou another?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, whence is it manifest that my sins are forgiven?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now he for his part utters no such word, but gives himself up to the power of the healer; but these being curious and envious, plot against the good deeds of others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore He rebukes them indeed, but with all gentleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Why, if ye disbelieve,” saith He, “what went before, and account my saying a boast; behold I add to it also another, the uncovering of your secrets; and after that again another.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The giving tone to the body of the paralyzed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And whereas, when He spake unto the sick of the palsy, He spake without clearly manifesting His own authority: for He said not, “I forgive thee thy sins,” but, “thy sins be forgiven thee:” upon their constraining, He discloses His authority more clearly, saying, “But that ye may know that the Son of Man hath poweron earth to forgive sins.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou, how far He was from unwillingness to be thought equal to the Father?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For He said not at all, “The Son of Man hath need of another;” or, “He hath given Him authority,” but, “He hath authority.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Neither doth He say it for love of honor, but “to convince you,” so He speaks, “that I do not blaspheme in making myself equal with God.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus everywhere His will is to offer proofs clear and indisputable; as when He saith, “Go thy way, show thyself to the priest;”and when He points to Peter’s wife’s mother ministering, and permits the swine to cast themselves down headlong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in the same manner here also; first, for a certain token of the forgiveness of his sins, He provides the giving tone to his body: and of that again, his carrying his bed; to hinder the fact from being thought a mere fancy.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He doeth not this, before He had asked them a question.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For whether is easier,” saith He, “to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee? or to say, Take up thy bed, and go unto thine house?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now what He saith is like this, “Which seems to you easier, to bind up a disorganizedbody, or to undothe sins of a soul?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: It is quite manifest; to bind up a body.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For by how much a soul is better than a body, by so much is the doing away sins a greater work than this; but because the one is unseen, the other in sight, I throw in that, which although an inferior thing, is yet more open to sense; that the greater also and the unseen may thereby receive its proof;” thus by His works anticipating even now the revelation of what had been said by John, that “He taketh away the sins of the world.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Well then, having raised him up, He sends him to his house; here again signifying His unboastfulness,and that the event was not a mere imagination; for He makes the same persons witnesses of his infirmity, and also of his health.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I indeed had desired, saith He, through thy calamity to heal those also, that seem to be in health, but are diseased in mind; but since they will not, depart thou home, to heal them that are there.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how He indicates Himto be Creator both of souls and bodies?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He heals therefore the palsy in each of the two substances, and makes the invisible evident by that which is in sight.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But nevertheless they still creep upon the earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For when the multitudes saw it, they marvelled, and glorified God, which” (it is said) “had given such power unto men:”for the flesh was an offense unto them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He did not rebuke them, but proceeds by His works to arouse them, and exalt their thoughts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since for the time it was no small thing for Him to be thought greater than all men, as having come from God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For had they well established these things in their own minds, going on orderly they would have known, that He was even the Son of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But they did not retain these things clearly, wherefore neither were they able to approach Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For they said again, “This man is not of God;”“how is this man of God?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they were continually harping on these things, putting them forward as cloaks for their own passions.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which thing many now also do; and thinking to avenge God, fulfill their own passions, when they ought to go about all with moderation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For even the God of all, having power to launch His thunderbolt against them that blaspheme Him, makes the sun to rise, and sends forth the showers, and affords them all other things in abundance; whom we ought to imitate, and so to entreat, advise, admonish, with meekness, not angry, not making ourselves wild beasts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For no harm at all ensues unto God by their blasphemy, that thou shouldest be angered, but he who blasphemed hath himself also received the wound.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore groan, bewail, for the calamity indeed deserves tears.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the wounded man, again,—noth ing can so heal him as gentleness: gentleness, I say, which is mightier than any force.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: See, for example, how He Himself, the insulted one, discourses with us, both in the Old Testament, and in the New; in the one saying, “O my people, what have I done unto thee?”in the other, “Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Paul too bids, “In meekness instruct those that oppose themselves.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And Christ again, when His disciples had come to Him, requiring fire to come down from heaven, strongly rebuked them, saying, “Ye know not what manner of spirit ye are of.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And here again He said not, “O accursed, and sorcerers as ye are; O ye envious, and enemies of men’s salvation;” but, “Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: We must, you see, use gentleness to eradicate the disease.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since he who is become better through the fear of man, will quickly return to wickedness again.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this cause He commanded also the tares to be left, giving an appointed day of repentance.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and many of them in fact repented, and became good, who before were bad; as for instance, Paul, the Publican, the Thief; for these being really tares turned into kindly wheat.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because, although in the seeds this cannot be, yet in the human will it is both manageable and easy; for our will is bound by no limits of nature, but hath freedom of choice for its privilege.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Accordingly, when thou seest an enemy of the truth, wait on him, take care of him, lead him back into virtue, by showing forth an excellent life, by applying “speech that cannot be condemned,”by bestowing attention and tender care, by trying every means of amendment, in imitation of the best physicians.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For neither do they cure in one manner only, but when they see the wound not yield to the first remedy, they add another, and after that again another; and now they use the knife, and now bind up.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And do thou accordingly, having become a physician of souls, put in practice every mode of cure according to Christ’s laws; that thou mayest receive the reward both of saving thyself and of profiting others, doing all to the glory of God, and so being glorified also thyself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For them that glorify me,” saith He, “I will glorify; and they that despise me, shall be lightly esteemed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us, I say, do all things unto His glory; that we may attain unto that blessed portion, unto which God grant we may all attain, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might forever and ever.
